_meta.doc: Denormalized per-kanji record file — joins n5_kanji_whitelist.json + n5_kanji_readings.json + kanji.json into one record per kanji. Generated by tools/build_n5_kanji_full.py (IMP-004, 2026-05-14). Runtime kanji-study UI can load THIS file instead of fetching + joining the three upstream files (which risked version drift between fetches). When any of the upstream files change, re-run the build script and commit the regenerated output.
see_also: data/kanji.json (canonical full metadata, single-kanji edits go HERE first)
data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json (membership canonical, kept stable for downstream indexers)

mnemonic.summary: A single horizontal stroke = ONE.
mnemonic.visual: A single horizontal stroke — ONE finger held up flat.
mnemonic.reading: いち / いつ — counting "ichi, ni, san" starts with ichi. Kun ひと appears in 一つ (hitotsu, one thing).
mnemonic.meaning: A single horizontal stroke = ONE.

sentences.ja: りんごは 一つしか ありません。

n5_compounds.gloss: one day, a whole day (note: same kanji 一日 with a d

stroke_order_trap.trap: 一 has only 1 stroke; learners sometimes forget the stroke is left-to-right.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: horizontal stroke (left → right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Direction matters even for single strokes — calligraphic convention is L→R, not R→L.

mnemonic.summary: A horizontal stroke crossed by a hook = SEVEN.
mnemonic.visual: Imagine the horizontal stroke as the SEA's surface, with the diagonal hook pulling down — SEVEN waves crashing under the surface. Or: a sickle (鎌) cutting downward through the air.
mnemonic.reading: しち / なな — "shichi" can be misheard, so なな is common spoken-language fallback.
mnemonic.meaning: A horizontal stroke crossed by a hook = SEVEN.

sentences.ja: えいがは 七時はんに はじまります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short horizontal stroke FIRST, then long curve down-and-up.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal (slanted slightly). 2: long curve (down + hook up).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Direction of stroke 2 (down then up) gives the calligraphic feel.

mnemonic.summary: A swooping line tied off = TEN THOUSAND.
mnemonic.visual: A flag with a pole and a swooping line — TEN THOUSAND flags waving in a parade.
mnemonic.reading: まん — used in 一万 (ichiman, 10,000). Watch the price tags for 万 to denote ten-thousand.
mnemonic.meaning: A swooping line tied off = TEN THOUSAND.

sentences.ja: 五万円 あります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 一 first, then ノ, then large hook.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal. 2: short ノ (left descending). 3: long hook (down + curl).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 3 strokes. Distinguishes from 方 (which has extra dot).

mnemonic.summary: Three horizontal strokes = THREE.
mnemonic.visual: Three horizontal strokes — THREE fingers raised.
mnemonic.reading: さん — borrowed everywhere from "Mr/Ms" (-さん) to 三月 (sangatsu, March). Kun み in 三つ (mittsu).
mnemonic.meaning: Three horizontal strokes = THREE.

sentences.ja: 本を 三さつ 読みました。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Same as 二 + middle stroke between them.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: middle horizontal. 3: long horizontal (bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Top-to-bottom stacking; middle is between, not last.

mnemonic.summary: A mark above the baseline = UP.
mnemonic.visual: A bar above the ground line — UP, ABOVE.
mnemonic.reading: じょう / うえ / あ / のぼ — 上 (ue, above), 上手 (jouzu, skilled), 上る (noboru, to climb).
mnemonic.meaning: A mark above the baseline = UP.

examples.gloss: top and bottom / up and down

sentences.ja: つくえの 上に 本が あります。
sentences.translation_en: There is a book on the desk.

sentences.ja: 本は つくえの 上に あります。
sentences.translation_en: The book is on the desk.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short horizontal FIRST, then vertical, then LONG horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal (top). 2: vertical (down). 3: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 3 strokes. Distinguishes from 下 (which starts with the long horizontal).

mnemonic.summary: A mark below the baseline = DOWN.
mnemonic.visual: A bar below the ground line — DOWN, BELOW.
mnemonic.reading: か / げ / した / さ / お — 下 (shita, below), 地下 (chika, underground).
mnemonic.meaning: A mark below the baseline = DOWN.

sentences.ja: 下では ありませんか。

stroke_order_trap.trap: LONG horizontal FIRST, then vertical, then dot.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: long horizontal (top). 2: vertical (down). 3: short dot (top-right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 3 strokes. Mirror-pair with 上 — STROKE 1 IS THE DIFFERENCE.

mnemonic.summary: A line through the middle of a box = MIDDLE.
mnemonic.visual: A box with a vertical line piercing through the MIDDLE — bull's eye.
mnemonic.reading: ちゅう / なか — 中国 (chuugoku, China), 中 (naka, inside).
mnemonic.meaning: A line through the middle of a box = MIDDLE.

sentences.ja: たぶん 田中さんは 学生だろう。
田中さんは 「行かない」って 言ってた。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Vertical stroke is LAST, not first.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: 口 left vertical. 2: 口 top + right (one stroke). 3: 口 bottom horizontal. 4: long central vertical (top to bottom through the box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: The vertical bisects the box, so the box must be drawn first to position the vertical correctly.

mnemonic.summary: A diagonal hook curling like the digit 9 = NINE.
mnemonic.visual: A hooked stroke meeting a vertical — NINE looks like a fishhook catching a fish.
mnemonic.reading: きゅう / く — "kyuu" used in counting, "ku" used in dates (九月 kugatsu, September).
mnemonic.meaning: A diagonal hook curling like the digit 9 = NINE.

sentences.ja: じかんは 五時から 九時までです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ stroke FIRST, then large arc stroke.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (left descending). 2: long arc (down-curve, up).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 九 has 2 strokes; stroke 2 is one continuous arc, not two.

mnemonic.summary: Two horizontal strokes = TWO.
mnemonic.visual: Two horizontal strokes — TWO chopsticks resting parallel.
mnemonic.reading: に — "ni" is the second beat in counting. Kun ふた lives in 二つ (futatsu, two things).
mnemonic.meaning: Two horizontal strokes = TWO.

sentences.ja: バスで 二十ぷん かかります。
お水を 二つ おねがいします。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal stroke FIRST, then bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal (top). 2: long horizontal (bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Top-first principle universal for stacked horizontals.

mnemonic.summary: Roman numeral V tilted between two horizontal lines = FIVE.
mnemonic.visual: A capital "I" with extra strokes — FIVE fingers locked in a tight grip.
mnemonic.reading: ご — quick and clean, as in 五月 (gogatsu, May). Kun いつ in 五つ (itsutsu).
mnemonic.meaning: Roman numeral V tilted between two horizontal lines = FIVE.

sentences.ja: シロは 五さいです。
じかんは 五時から 九時までです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal, then vertical, then bottom-curving stroke, then close at bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: vertical+L-shape (one stroke). 3: middle horizontal. 4: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes total. The 2nd stroke is L-shaped (one continuous), not two separate.

mnemonic.summary: Two legs walking = a PERSON.
mnemonic.visual: Two strokes leaning into each other — a PERSON walking on two legs.
mnemonic.reading: じん / にん / ひと — country + 人 = nationality (日本人 nihonjin). Kun ひと: あの人 (ano hito).
mnemonic.meaning: Two legs walking = a PERSON.

sentences.ja: おすしを たべる 人
べんきょうしない 人。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 人 starts with the LONG stroke (left-descending), opposite of 入.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: long ノ (left-descending). 2: short ╲ (right-descending, joining mid-stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Reversed order produces 入. The crossing-point also shifts.

mnemonic.summary: A person under a roof at this very instant = NOW.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over a small mark — sheltering RIGHT NOW under the present moment.
mnemonic.reading: こん / いま — 今日 (kyou, today!) and 今週 (konshuu, this week). Kun いま: 今 (ima, now).
mnemonic.meaning: A person under a roof at this very instant = NOW.

sentences.ja: 今日は 行きたくないです。
sentences.translation_en: I don't want to go today.

sentences.ja: 今日は 雨なので、行きません。
sentences.translation_en: Since it's raining today, I won't go.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top is "hat" 个 (3 strokes); bottom is just 7-shape.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: 个-top: ノ (left). 2: ╲ (right). 3: ─ (horizontal under hat). 4: 7-shape (one stroke, hooks left).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes total. Common error: writing the bottom as 2 separate strokes.

mnemonic.summary: A person (亻) leaning on a tree (木) = REST.
mnemonic.visual: A person (亻) leaning against a tree (木) — REST against the trunk.
mnemonic.reading: きゅう / やす — 休む (yasumu, to rest). 休日 (kyuujitsu, day off).
mnemonic.meaning: A person (亻) leaning on a tree (木) = REST.

examples.gloss: on a break / during a holiday

sentences.ja: 休みが ほしいです。
なつ休みは なんがつからですか。
sentences.translation_en: When (what month) does summer vacation start?

n5_compounds.gloss: rest, holiday (noun form of やすむ)

stroke_order_trap.trap: Drawing 木 (the right element) before 亻 (the left radical, にんべん). All にんべん kanji are left-first: finish 亻 (2 strokes), then start 木.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-2: 亻 (ノ + vertical). 3-6: 木.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes. Left-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: People (人) gathering in clouds (云) = MEET.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over 云 cloud — people MEETING under a roof, MEETING at the cloud level.
mnemonic.reading: かい / あ — 会社 (kaisha, company), 会議 (kaigi, meeting). Kun あ: 会う (au, to meet).
mnemonic.meaning: People (人) gathering in clouds (云) = MEET.

sentences.ja:
ヤマダさんへ
あした 1時に えきの まえで 会いましょう。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Many learners draw the bottom 云 first because it's the more prominent element. The top 人-hat (ノ then ╲) must come first — the same rule as 入 / 八 / 全.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left ノ. 2: right ╲ (hat). 3-6: 云 (top horizontal, dot, hook+curl).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes.

mnemonic.summary: A person (亻) asking what is possible (可) = WHAT.
mnemonic.visual: A person (亻) + 可 can — when a person CAN'T name something, they ask "WHAT?"
mnemonic.reading: なに / なん — なに alone (何ですか) but なん before some sounds (何時, nanji). Watch the shift!
mnemonic.meaning: A person (亻) asking what is possible (可) = WHAT.

sentences.ja: 何時に きましたか。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Person-radical 亻 first (left), then 可 right.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-2: 亻 (left side: ノ + vertical). 3-7: 可 (top horizontal, mouth-like, hook).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Left-radical-first rule.

mnemonic.summary: A person (儿) striding ahead = PREVIOUS / earlier.
mnemonic.visual: A leg stepping forward, leading the way — going FIRST and AHEAD.
mnemonic.reading: せん / さき — 先生 (sensei, teacher = "born ahead"). Kun さき: 先 (saki, ahead).
mnemonic.meaning: A person (儿) striding ahead = PREVIOUS / earlier.

sentences.ja: 先生に なりたいです。
sentences.translation_en: I want to become a teacher.

sentences.ja: 田中さんは 先生です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top 牛-like shape first, then 儿 (legs) below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: top horizontal. 3: short horizontal. 4: vertical. 5: 儿 left leg. 6: 儿 right hook.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes; 儿 (legs) always at the end.

mnemonic.summary: Two strokes meeting at a point - going INSIDE = ENTER.
mnemonic.visual: Two strokes meeting at a point — entering through a doorway, GOING IN.
mnemonic.reading: にゅう / い / はい — 入る (hairu, to enter). 入口 (iriguchi, entrance), 入学 (nyuugaku).
mnemonic.meaning: Two strokes meeting at a point - going INSIDE = ENTER.

examples.gloss: to enter (Group 1 exception - looks like Group 2)

sentences.ja: ここに 入るな。
どうぞ 入ってください。

n5_compounds.gloss: to enter (Group 1 exception - looks like Group 2)

stroke_order_trap.trap: 入 starts with the SHORT stroke (left-descending), 人 starts with the LONG one — easy to swap.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (left-descending, with curl at bottom). 2: long ╲ (right-descending).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Visual identity vs 人: 入 has the short-then-long order; 人 has long-then-short. Reversed order produces the wrong character.

mnemonic.summary: Two strokes splitting outward like a fan = EIGHT.
mnemonic.visual: Two strokes splaying outward — EIGHT legs of an OCTOPUS spread wide.
mnemonic.reading: はち — sharp like a bee sting (hachi = bee in Japanese!), and 8 hours is はちじかん.
mnemonic.meaning: Two strokes splitting outward like a fan = EIGHT.

sentences.ja: でも、電車が おくれて、八時十分に 学校に つきました。

日にち：八月一日から 八月七日まで
時間：あさ 十時から よる 八時まで
本は ぜんぶ 半分の ねだんです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 八 has TWO separate diagonal strokes that DO NOT touch at the top.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left descending stroke (slants outward). 2: right descending stroke (slants outward).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Strokes flare apart in 八 (eight) but converge to a peak in 人/入. Touching the top makes it look like 人.

mnemonic.summary: A lid (亠) over a split (八) = SIX.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over a pair of legs — SIX is the legs of a SIX-pack of beer under a tavern roof.
mnemonic.reading: ろく — like rolling a SIX on a die: "rock and roll" → roku.
mnemonic.meaning: A lid (亠) over a split (八) = SIX.

sentences.ja: 父も 六時に 来ます。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top dot first, THEN long horizontal, THEN ハ (two legs).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top dot. 2: long horizontal. 3: left ノ leg. 4: right ╲ leg.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Dot-first convention for hat-style structures. Distinguishes 六 from 大.

mnemonic.summary: Inside the box (冂) is earth (土) = round YEN coin.
mnemonic.visual: A box with rounded inside — a coin in a wallet pocket. ROUND like the YEN coin.
mnemonic.reading: えん — every yen price ends in 円. Also means "round" — an えんりょ-shaped coin.
mnemonic.meaning: Inside the box (冂) is earth (土) = round YEN coin.

sentences.ja: 5,000円くらいです。
1,000円だけ あります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Standard box rule with 月-like enclosure: left vertical, top+right, inside cross, close bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical (with hook). 2: top + right bracket (with hook). 3-4: 二 inside. (4 strokes total for 円).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Outer-frame first; inside stays bare in 円 (different from 田).

mnemonic.summary: Stacked containers - sticking OUT.
mnemonic.visual: A mountain on top of a mountain — climbing OUT of the valley.
mnemonic.reading: しゅつ / すい / で / だ — 出る (deru, to leave). 出口 (deguchi, exit), 出発 (shuppatsu).
mnemonic.meaning: Stacked containers - sticking OUT.

sentences.ja: 出かける まえに かさを もちます。
sentences.translation_en: I take an umbrella before going out.

sentences.ja: 出かける まえに、しんぶんを 読みます。
sentences.translation_en: Before going out, I read the newspaper.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Bottom 山 first, then top 山 — built from the inside out.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: middle vertical (top half). 2: top-left mini-hook. 3: top horizontal closing top-山. 4: middle vertical (bottom half — extends down from top). 5: bottom-left mini-hook of bottom-山.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: NOT two separate 山 stacked; the central verticals connect, making it 5 strokes (not 6+).

mnemonic.summary: Splitting (八) with a knife (刀) = MINUTE / divide.
mnemonic.visual: Eight (八) over sword (刀) — divide a thing by cutting eight ways. A MINUTE is a divided slice of an hour.
mnemonic.reading: ふん / ぷん / ぶん — minutes shift voicing: 一分 (ippun), 三分 (sanpun). Also ぶん in 自分 (jibun, oneself).
mnemonic.meaning: Splitting (八) with a knife (刀) = MINUTE / divide.

sentences.ja: 10分ぐらい かかります。
1日に 30分ずつ べんきょうします。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top ハ (ノ+ ╲), then 刀 (Japanese sword) below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left ノ (descending). 2: right ╲ (descending). 3: 刀 top horizontal. 4: 刀 curve+hook (one stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes total; the bottom 刀 is 2 strokes only.

mnemonic.summary: In FRONT, knife (刂) ready, sharing meat (月) with a smile (丷一).
mnemonic.visual: Horns (or a moon) in BEFORE-sky — what comes BEFORE.
mnemonic.reading: ぜん / まえ — 前 (mae, in front). 午前 (gozen, AM), 前回 (zenkai, previous time).
mnemonic.meaning: In FRONT, knife (刂) ready, sharing meat (月) with a smile (丷一).

sentences.ja: ここに 名前を 書いてください。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top 八 (dots), middle horizontal, then 月 + 刂.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot. 2: right dot. 3: horizontal. 4-7: 月 (frame). 8-9: 刂 (vertical + hook).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 9 strokes. Three-component arrangement.

mnemonic.summary: A flexed arm muscle = POWER.
mnemonic.visual: A bicep flexed — pure muscle POWER.
mnemonic.reading: りょく / ちから — 力 (chikara, strength). 体力 (tairyoku, physical strength).
mnemonic.meaning: A flexed arm muscle = POWER.

sentences.ja: 力を かして ください。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Hook stroke first (top to bottom-right curl), then ノ.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: hook (downward + curl right). 2: ノ (descending-left, crosses).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 2 strokes. Stroke order distinguishes from 九 (which has horizontal at top).

mnemonic.summary: Two figures back-to-back facing away from the cold = NORTH.
mnemonic.visual: Two figures back-to-back — turning their backs to the cold NORTH wind.
mnemonic.reading: ほく / きた — 北 (kita, north). 東北 (Touhoku, the northeast region).
mnemonic.meaning: Two figures back-to-back facing away from the cold = NORTH.

sentences.ja: 男: 北の 出口です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Left side (匕 mirrored), then right 匕.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: vertical (left). 2: ╲ (left side). 3: ノ (right). 4: hook (right). 5: bottom curve.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. The mirror symmetry is misleading — different stroke counts each half.

mnemonic.summary: A plus sign - Roman numeral X rotated = TEN.
mnemonic.visual: A perfect cross — TEN is two roads meeting; tally bars at TEN strokes.
mnemonic.reading: じゅう / じっ — "juu" stretches to mean "complete." Used in 十時 (juuji, 10 o'clock).
mnemonic.meaning: A plus sign - Roman numeral X rotated = TEN.

sentences.ja: バスで 二十ぷん かかります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Horizontal stroke FIRST, then vertical.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: horizontal stroke. 2: vertical stroke (top to bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Universal in 十-shape kanji components.

mnemonic.summary: A slash (丿) over ten (十) = a THOUSAND tally marks.
mnemonic.visual: A slash crossing a ten — like THOUSAND sticks falling at a slight angle.
mnemonic.reading: せん — bills come in 千円 notes (sen-en). Kun ち in names like 千葉 (Chiba).
mnemonic.meaning: A slash (丿) over ten (十) = a THOUSAND tally marks.

sentences.ja: あの 大きい 本は 二千円です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ at top first, then horizontal, then vertical.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (left descending). 2: horizontal. 3: long vertical (top to bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Distinguishes 千 from 干 (干 starts with horizontal).

mnemonic.summary: A pestle pointing down at noon = NOON.
mnemonic.visual: A horizontal line with a pestle below — at NOON the sun pounds straight down on the ground.
mnemonic.reading: ご — only in 午前 (gozen, AM) and 午後 (gogo, PM). Solid ご.
mnemonic.meaning: A pestle pointing down at noon = NOON.

sentences.ja: <u>午前</u> くじに がっこうへ いきます。
<u>午後</u> さんじに あいましょう。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ at top, then horizontal, then full vertical (top-down).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: top horizontal. 3: short horizontal (middle). 4: long vertical.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes. Often confused with 牛 (which has extra vertical).

mnemonic.summary: Mark cutting in two = HALF.
mnemonic.visual: Eight on top + cow below cut down the middle — HALF of a cow.
mnemonic.reading: はん — 半分 (hanbun, half) and 三時半 (sanji-han, 3:30). Always はん.
mnemonic.meaning: Mark cutting in two = HALF.

sentences.ja:
日にち：八月一日から 八月七日まで
時間：あさ 十時から よる 八時まで
本は ぜんぶ 半分の ねだんです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top ハ-dots first, then horizontal, then vertical (last).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot. 2: right dot. 3: middle horizontal. 4: long bottom horizontal. 5: vertical (through).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Vertical LAST is the trap; many write vertical first.

mnemonic.summary: A bell-shape facing the warm sun direction = SOUTH.
mnemonic.visual: A roof, a samurai banner — banners flying SOUTH in summer wind.
mnemonic.reading: なん / みなみ — 南 (minami, south). 南極 (nankyoku, Antarctica).
mnemonic.meaning: A bell-shape facing the warm sun direction = SOUTH.

sentences.ja: 南の くにに 行きました。
sentences.translation_en: I went to a southern country.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top dot+horizontal, then complex inside with 干-like + box.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top dot+horizontal. 2-3: outer frame strokes. 4-9: inside elements. Total 9.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 9 strokes. Multi-tier structure.

mnemonic.summary: Two hands clasped = FRIEND.
mnemonic.visual: Two crossed hands — FRIENDS shaking hands or high-fiving.
mnemonic.reading: ゆう / とも — 友達 (tomodachi, friend). On ゆう in 友人 (yuujin).
mnemonic.meaning: Two hands clasped = FRIEND.

sentences.ja: 友だちの たんじょうびです。
sentences.translation_en: It's my friend's birthday. (noun-modifier の: links possessor to event)

sentences.ja: 友だちが 本を くれました。
sentences.translation_en: A friend gave me a book.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 一 first (top diagonal), then 又.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: ノ (top-left descent). 3: 又 (right curve+ノ — 2 strokes).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Distinguishes from 反 (similar shape).

mnemonic.summary: A square opening = MOUTH.
mnemonic.visual: A square box opening — an open MOUTH.
mnemonic.reading: こう / くち / ぐち — 入り口 (iriguchi, entrance), 人口 (jinkou, population).
mnemonic.meaning: A square opening = MOUTH.

sentences.ja: えきの 出口を 出て、まっすぐ 行きます。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Bottom horizontal LAST, not first. Common mistake: drawing four sides clockwise from top.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top + right (one bracket stroke). 3: bottom horizontal (closes box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Three-stroke kanji, NOT four. The top+right is a single stroke (hook).

mnemonic.summary: Ten (十) generations of stories (口) = OLD.
mnemonic.visual: Ten (十) + mouth (口) — ten generations of mouths talking: this is OLD news.
mnemonic.reading: こ / ふる — 古い (furui, old). 中古 (chuuko, used/secondhand).
mnemonic.meaning: Ten (十) generations of stories (口) = OLD.

sentences.ja: きょうとは 古い まちです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Writing 口 first is the common mistake. The 十 on top (horizontal then vertical) precedes the 口 box — top-down assembly. Inside 口, follow standard box rule (left side / top+right bracket / bottom horizontal).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: horizontal. 2: vertical. 3-5: 口 (with box rule).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. Cross + box.

mnemonic.summary: A hand (ナ) over a mouth (口) - natural right-hand bite = RIGHT.
mnemonic.visual: A hand bringing food to the mouth — your RIGHT hand eats for you.
mnemonic.reading: ゆう / う / みぎ — 右 (migi, right). 右側 (migigawa, right side).
mnemonic.meaning: A hand (ナ) over a mouth (口) - natural right-hand bite = RIGHT.

sentences.ja: はじめの しんごうを 右に まがります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 右 starts with the SHORT stroke (descending-left); 左 starts with the LONG horizontal — easy to swap.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (descending-left). 2: long horizontal. 3-5: 口 (left, top-right, bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Mirror-pair with 左; opposite stroke 1 is the only visual cue distinguishing the calligraphic balance.

mnemonic.summary: Mouth (口) shouting a NUMBER / call sign.
mnemonic.visual: Mouth (口) + bent breath — calling out a NUMBER/CALL-SIGN.
mnemonic.reading: ごう — 番号 (bangou, number), 信号 (shingou, traffic signal). Always ごう.
mnemonic.meaning: Mouth (口) shouting a NUMBER / call sign.

sentences.ja: 一号せんに のります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Writing the bottom 丂 before completing 口 is the typical mistake. The structure is top-down: 口 (3 strokes) first, then 丂 (horizontal then hook+curl). Same top-first rule as 員 / 品.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 口. 4: top of 丂 (horizontal). 5: 丂 hook+curl.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes.

mnemonic.summary: In the evening (夕), call out (口) the NAME.
mnemonic.visual: Evening (夕) + mouth (口) — at evening you speak your NAME.
mnemonic.reading: めい / みょう / な — 名前 (namae, name). 有名 (yuumei, famous).
mnemonic.meaning: In the evening (夕), call out (口) the NAME.

sentences.ja: 名前は シロです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top is 夕 (3 strokes), then 口 (3 strokes) bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 夕 (evening). 4-6: 口 (mouth) with closing-bottom rule.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Common error: writing 口 first because it is more familiar.

mnemonic.summary: Mouth (口) over shell-money (貝) - a paid speaker = MEMBER / employee.
mnemonic.visual: Mouth (口) over 貝 shell-money — the MEMBER who counts shells with mouth open.
mnemonic.reading: いん — 会社員 (kaishain), 駅員 (ekiin, station-staff), 店員 (tenin, shop clerk).
mnemonic.meaning: Mouth (口) over shell-money (貝) - a paid speaker = MEMBER / employee.

sentences.ja: 店員が 話して います。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 口 (top) + 貝 (bottom: see, valuables).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 口 (top box). 4-10: 貝 (eye-shape + two legs).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 10 strokes. Top component first.

mnemonic.summary: Four legs (儿) inside a box = FOUR.
mnemonic.visual: A box with two legs sticking down — FOUR legs of a table inside a frame.
mnemonic.reading: し / よん — "shi" sounds like death so よん is preferred when counting people. Both readings are common.
mnemonic.meaning: Four legs (儿) inside a box = FOUR.

sentences.ja: 土ようびは 一時から 四時までです。
中国人は 四人で、かんこく人も 四人 います。
sentences.translation_en: There are four Chinese people, and four Korean people too.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Box stroke order: left, top+right (one stroke), inside, then close at bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top+right (bracket). 3-4: inside (two short ノ). 5: bottom horizontal (closes).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Box-closure rule: enclose, fill inside, close LAST.

mnemonic.summary: A jewel (玉) inside borders (囗) = COUNTRY.
mnemonic.visual: Border (囗) around 玉 jade — a COUNTRY is a treasure surrounded by a border.
mnemonic.reading: こく / ごく / くに — 国 (kuni, country). 中国 (chuugoku, China).
mnemonic.meaning: A jewel (玉) inside borders (囗) = COUNTRY.

sentences.ja: なつ休みに 国へ かえるつもりです。
sentences.translation_en: I intend to go back home for summer break.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Outside frame opens BEFORE inside; bottom of OUTER frame closes LAST.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: outer left vertical. 2: outer top+right bracket. 3-7: inside 玉 strokes. 8: outer bottom (closes frame).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Universal rule for enclosed kanji: enclose first, fill inside, close last.

mnemonic.summary: A cross stuck in the ground = EARTH.
mnemonic.visual: A cross with a bottom line — a stake driven into the EARTH with the line marking ground level.
mnemonic.reading: ど / つち — Saturday is どようび. Kun つち: 土 (tsuchi, soil).
mnemonic.meaning: A cross stuck in the ground = EARTH.

examples.gloss: on top of the dirt / on the ground

sentences.ja: 土曜日は ともだちと サッカーを します。
土曜日に 母と さくらを 見に 行きます。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal, then vertical, then bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: vertical (top to bottom). 3: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Same as 王 family but without middle horizontal.

mnemonic.summary: Evening (夕) divination (卜) - done OUTSIDE the home.
mnemonic.visual: Evening (夕) + divination (卜) — fortune-telling done OUTSIDE.
mnemonic.reading: がい / げ / そと / ほか — 外国 (gaikoku, foreign country), 外 (soto, outside).
mnemonic.meaning: Evening (夕) divination (卜) - done OUTSIDE the home.

sentences.ja: きょうは 雨ですから、外へ いき （　　）。
きょうは さむいので、<u>外</u> へ でません。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 夕 (left), then 卜 (divination, right).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 夕. 4: vertical. 5: short top-right dot.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes.

mnemonic.summary: A person with arms stretched wide = BIG.
mnemonic.visual: A person (人) with arms outstretched — "It was THIS BIG!"
mnemonic.reading: だい / たい / おお — 大学 (daigaku, university), 大きい (ookii, big).
mnemonic.meaning: A person with arms stretched wide = BIG.

examples.note: Irregular jukujikun reading: 大 here is not pronounced as either おお, だい, or たい.

examples.gloss: big (variant adjectival form of 大きい)

sentences.ja: この くつは 大きすぎます。
あんな 大きい いえに すみたいです。
sentences.translation_en: I want to live in that kind of big house.

n5_compounds.gloss: big (variant adjectival form of 大きい)

stroke_order_trap.trap: The dot of 太 vs 犬 vs 大 lives in different positions; 大 has NO dot.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: long ノ (left-down). 3: long ╲ (right-down).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Adding any dot turns 大 into either 太 (dot below crossing) or 犬 (dot at top-right).

mnemonic.summary: The biggest thing (大) under the line (一) = HEAVEN / sky.
mnemonic.visual: A person (大) reaching above — touching the SKY/HEAVENS.
mnemonic.reading: てん / あま / あめ — 天気 (tenki, weather), 天才 (tensai, genius).
mnemonic.meaning: The biggest thing (大) under the line (一) = HEAVEN / sky.

examples.gloss: the Milky Way (lit. river of heaven)

sentences.ja: きょうは いい 天気だなあ。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top short stroke, then long horizontal, then 大-like below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal (short). 2: long horizontal. 3: ノ (left-descending). 4: ╲ (right-descending).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes. Distinguishes from 大 (which has only ONE horizontal at top).

mnemonic.summary: A figure with crossed legs sitting gracefully = WOMAN.
mnemonic.visual: A figure with crossed legs gracefully kneeling — a WOMAN sitting in seiza.
mnemonic.reading: じょ / おんな — 女の人 (onna no hito, woman). 女性 (josei, female).
mnemonic.meaning: A figure with crossed legs sitting gracefully = WOMAN.

sentences.ja: 女の人が 男の人と 話しています。
sentences.translation_en: A woman is talking to a man.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Cross stroke comes LAST, not first.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: ノ (left descending) curving. 2: bottom horizontal (extending right). 3: long horizontal across (cross stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Cross-last is opposite of intuition; if drawn first, balance is off.

mnemonic.summary: A baby with arms outstretched and swaddled = CHILD.
mnemonic.visual: A baby with arms out and legs swaddled — a CHILD bundled tight.
mnemonic.reading: し / こ — kun こ in 子供 (kodomo, child). On し in 男子 (danshi).
mnemonic.meaning: A baby with arms outstretched and swaddled = CHILD.

examples.gloss: child (often written こども in everyday text)

sentences.ja: こうえんに 子どもが いますか。
sentences.translation_en: Are there children in the park?

sentences.ja: 子どもたちに あめを 二つずつ あげました。
sentences.translation_en: I gave each child two candies.

n5_compounds.gloss: child (often written こども in everyday text)

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top curl is ONE stroke (cross-curve), then vertical hook, then bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: 7-shape top (right-then-curve-down). 2: vertical hook (long down with curl at bottom). 3: long horizontal across.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 3 strokes total. Common error: drawing top as separate strokes (4-stroke version).

mnemonic.summary: A child (子) under a roof (冖) with knowledge crowning down = STUDY.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over a child reaching for knowledge — STUDYING under a school.
mnemonic.reading: がく / まな — 学校 (gakkou, school), 学生 (gakusei, student). Kun まな: 学ぶ (manabu).
mnemonic.meaning: A child (子) under a roof (冖) with knowledge crowning down = STUDY.

sentences.ja: バスで 学校へ 行きます。
sentences.translation_en: I go to school by bus.

sentences.ja: もう 学校に 行きました。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top hat-element (3 dots), then 冖 (cover), then 子.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: top dots (3 dots in slight V). 4-6: 冖 cover (horizontal + vertical+hook). 7-8: 子 below.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes total. Three-tier kanji.

mnemonic.summary: A woman (女) under a roof (宀) - peaceful and CHEAP.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over a woman — woman safe at home: PEACEFUL, also CHEAP.
mnemonic.reading: あん / やす — 安い (yasui, cheap). 安心 (anshin, peace of mind).
mnemonic.meaning: A woman (女) under a roof (宀) - peaceful and CHEAP.

sentences.ja: コーヒーが おいしくて、 ねだんも 安いです。
コーヒーが おいしくて、 ねだんも 安いです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Drawing 女 before fully closing 宀 (うかんむり) is one slip; the other is reversing 女's own internal order — 女's long horizontal cross-stroke comes LAST, not first.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 宀 (dot, horizontal+hook). 4-6: 女 (cross-stroke last in 女).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes. Roof-first.

mnemonic.summary: Three small specks scattered = SMALL.
mnemonic.visual: A central stroke with two flecks — SMALL grains of rice on either side.
mnemonic.reading: しょう / ちい / こ / お — 小さい (chiisai, small), 小学校 (shougakkou, primary school).
mnemonic.meaning: Three small specks scattered = SMALL.

examples.gloss: small stream / brook (also a common surname)

sentences.ja: ねこは いぬより 小さいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: CENTER stroke first (vertical with hook), then dots.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: center vertical-with-hook (down then curl up-left). 2: left dot. 3: right dot.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Center-first is opposite of typical left-right intuition; getting it right makes the kanji symmetric.

mnemonic.summary: Three peaks of a MOUNTAIN range.
mnemonic.visual: Three peaks rising in a row — MOUNTAIN ridge.
mnemonic.reading: さん / やま — 富士山 (Fujisan, Mt. Fuji), 山 (yama, mountain).
mnemonic.meaning: Three peaks of a MOUNTAIN range.

sentences.ja: 高い 山が 見えます。
sentences.translation_en: I can see a tall mountain.

sentences.ja: 高い 山が 見えます。
sentences.translation_en: I can see a tall mountain.

stroke_order_trap.trap: CENTER vertical first, then left/right verticals.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: center tall vertical. 2: bottom-left vertical+horizontal (one stroke). 3: right vertical (slight inward slant).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Center-first is unusual; many learners draw left-to-right and produce uneven peaks.

mnemonic.summary: Three flowing lines of a RIVER.
mnemonic.visual: Three flowing vertical strokes — water FLOWING DOWN a RIVER.
mnemonic.reading: せん / かわ / がわ — 川 (kawa, river). 川辺 (kawabe, riverside).
mnemonic.meaning: Three flowing lines of a RIVER.

sentences.ja: <u>川</u> で さかなを みました。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Strokes go from LEFT to RIGHT in 川 (NOT center first like 山).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical (slight curve). 2: middle vertical (straight). 3: right vertical (with hook).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 川 (river) and 山 (mountain) look similar but stroke order differs; 川 is left-to-right "flowing", 山 is center-out "peaks".

mnemonic.summary: A hand (ナ) holding a tool (工) - natural left-hand grip = LEFT.
mnemonic.visual: A hand sweeping left over a workbench — your LEFT hand at the workshop.
mnemonic.reading: さ / ひだり — 左 (hidari, left). 左右 (sayuu, left and right).
mnemonic.meaning: A hand (ナ) holding a tool (工) - natural left-hand grip = LEFT.

sentences.ja: 左に ゆうびんきょくが あります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 左 starts with the LONG horizontal first (opposite of 右).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: long horizontal. 2: short ノ (descending-left). 3-5: 工 (work) bottom.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Mirror-pair with 右; getting stroke 1 right is the calligraphic identity of left-vs-right.

mnemonic.summary: A stalk grown over time = a YEAR.
mnemonic.visual: A person leaning on a stick — an old man carrying the weight of YEARS.
mnemonic.reading: ねん / とし — 一年 (ichinen, one year). Kun とし: 今年 (kotoshi, this year).
mnemonic.meaning: A stalk grown over time = a YEAR.

examples.reading: まいとし / まいねん
examples.gloss: every year (まいとし is more common in conversation; まいねん in formal/written contexts)

sentences.ja: わたしは 1年前から 日本語を べんきょうしています。
来年 日本に 行きたいです。

n5_compounds.reading: まいとし / まいねん
n5_compounds.gloss: every year (まいとし is more common in conversation; ま

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ, then horizontal, then second horizontal, then vertical+horizontal underneath.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: short horizontal (top). 3: longer horizontal (middle). 4: vertical (down through). 5: horizontal across vertical. 6: bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes total. Common confusion: skipping the short horizontal.

mnemonic.summary: Under a roof (广), claim a spot (占) = SHOP.
mnemonic.visual: Shed (广) + 占 fortune — fortune-tellers SET UP SHOP under a roof.
mnemonic.reading: てん / みせ — 店 (mise, shop). 店員 (tenin, shop clerk), 喫茶店 (kissaten, café).
mnemonic.meaning: Under a roof (广), claim a spot (占) = SHOP.

sentences.ja: 母: 店で 何を 買いますか。
店で コーヒーを 買いたいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 广 cap (3 strokes), then 占 inside.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 广 (dot + horizontal + ノ). 4-8: 占 (vertical + 卜 + 口).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes. Roof-cap-first structure.

mnemonic.summary: A traveler (彳) trailing behind = AFTER.
mnemonic.visual: Walking radical + thread + bottom — the THREAD comes BEHIND on the path.
mnemonic.reading: ご / こう / あと / うし / おく — 後 (ato, after). 午後 (gogo, PM), 後ろ (ushiro, behind).
mnemonic.meaning: A traveler (彳) trailing behind = AFTER.

sentences.ja: どうして ハイキングは 後の 日に なりましたか。
この 人は そつぎょうした 後、何を しますか。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 彳 (left radical, 3 strokes) first, then complex right side.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 彳. 4: 幺-like top. 5-9: bottom ⼡-like.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 9 strokes. Left-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: Five fingers spreading from a wrist = HAND.
mnemonic.visual: A hand with three fingers and a thumb — HAND raised.
mnemonic.reading: しゅ / て — 手 (te, hand) — direct and frequent. On しゅ in 手段 (shudan, method).
mnemonic.meaning: Five fingers spreading from a wrist = HAND.

sentences.ja: だれか 手つだってください。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ FIRST, then two horizontals, then vertical with hook.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: top horizontal. 3: middle horizontal. 4: long vertical with hook.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes; the final hook is a curl, not separate.

mnemonic.summary: A standing tree freshly cut by an axe = NEW.
mnemonic.visual: Stand (立) + tree (木) + axe (斤) — chopping NEW wood with a fresh axe.
mnemonic.reading: しん / あたら / あら / にい — 新しい (atarashii, new). 新聞 (shinbun, newspaper).
mnemonic.meaning: A standing tree freshly cut by an axe = NEW.

sentences.ja: 新しい くるまが ほしいです。
新しい かばんが ほしいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 亲 (left side, 9 strokes) + 斤 (right, 4 strokes).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 13 strokes. Left part first.

mnemonic.summary: A box with a stroke inside = the SUN, a DAY.
mnemonic.visual: A box with a line through the middle — the SUN at noon, light splitting the sky.
mnemonic.reading: にち / じつ / ひ / び — Sunday is にちようび. The kun ひ appears in 日 (hi, day) and 毎日 (mainichi, every day).
mnemonic.meaning: A box with a stroke inside = the SUN, a DAY.

sentences.ja: 友だちが 来る 日
日本語の 先生です。

n5_compounds.gloss: one day, a whole day (note: same kanji 一日 with a d

stroke_order_trap.trap: Bottom horizontal closes the box LAST.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top + right (bracket). 3: middle horizontal. 4: bottom horizontal (closes box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Same convention as 田/口/国: enclose first, close last. Common to most "box" kanji.

mnemonic.summary: Sun (日) + temple (寺, time-keeper) = TIME / hour.
mnemonic.visual: Sun (日) + temple (寺) — the temple bell rings out the TIME of day.
mnemonic.reading: じ / とき — clock times: 三時 (sanji, 3 o'clock). Kun とき: そのとき (sono toki, at that time).
mnemonic.meaning: Sun (日) + temple (寺, time-keeper) = TIME / hour.

sentences.ja: いま 3時です。
7時に おきます。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 日 first (left), then 寺 right side: 土 then 寸.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: 日 (left, top+right, middle, bottom). 5-7: 土 (top horizontal, vertical, bottom). 8-10: 寸 (horizontal, vertical, dot).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Left radical first, then right side top-to-bottom.

mnemonic.summary: Sun (日) + feathers (羽) + bird (隹) - DAY OF THE WEEK named after a celestial body.
mnemonic.visual: Sun (日) + wings + bird (隹) — the day BIRD soars across the SKY each WEEKDAY.
mnemonic.reading: よう — only meaningful in 曜日 (youbi, day-of-week). Memorize the chunk: 月曜日 / 火曜日 ...
mnemonic.meaning: Sun (日) + feathers (羽) + bird (隹) - DAY OF THE WEEK named after a celestial body.

sentences.ja: 日曜日は 学校に 行かなくても いいです。
sentences.translation_en: You don't have to go to school on Sundays.

sentences.ja: 日曜日は そうじしたり、せんたくしたり します。
sentences.translation_en: On Sundays I clean, do laundry, and so on.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Complex character: left 日, then top-right (羽-like), then bottom 隹.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: 日 (box). 5-10: top羽 element. 11-18: 隹 element. Total 18 strokes.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Multi-component kanji follow component-by-component rule.

mnemonic.summary: A brush over a writing surface in daylight = WRITE / book.
mnemonic.visual: Brush (聿) + 日 page — a BRUSH WRITING on the page.
mnemonic.reading: しょ / か — 書く (kaku, to write). 図書館 (toshokan, library).
mnemonic.meaning: A brush over a writing surface in daylight = WRITE / book.

sentences.ja: この かんじは どうやって 書きますか。
sentences.translation_en: How do you write this kanji?

sentences.ja: 母は かんじを 書くのが じょうずです。
sentences.translation_en: My mother is good at writing.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Starting with the bottom 曰 is the typical mistake. The order is the top horizontal first, then the 聿-like upper block top-to-bottom, then 曰 last. Mirror of the more visible bottom-half.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal. 2-7: 聿-like upper part. 8-10: 曰 box. Total 10.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 10 strokes. Top-to-bottom assembly.

mnemonic.summary: A crescent shape = the MOON, a MONTH.
mnemonic.visual: A crescent shape with two horizontal lines — the MOON inside a window frame.
mnemonic.reading: げつ / つき — Monday is げつようび. Kun つき: 月 (tsuki, moon).
mnemonic.meaning: A crescent shape = the MOON, a MONTH.

sentences.ja: 今は 4月です。
あいています：月〜金の 9時から 17時まで。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Left vertical FIRST (with hook); then frame; then two inside horizontals.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical (with bottom-left hook). 2: top+right (bracket with hook). 3: middle horizontal. 4: bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: The hook on stroke 1 distinguishes 月 from 日 (no hook); frame-second is enclosed-shape rule.

mnemonic.summary: A trunk with branches and roots = TREE.
mnemonic.visual: A vertical trunk with branches outward and roots downward — a TREE.
mnemonic.reading: もく / き — Thursday is もくようび. Kun き: 木 (ki, tree); also 木曜日 (mokuyoubi).
mnemonic.meaning: A trunk with branches and roots = TREE.

sentences.ja: 木の つくえです。
sentences.translation_en: It is a wooden desk. (noun-modifier の: links a material to an object)

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal first, THEN vertical (not vertical first).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: long central vertical. 3: ノ (left-descending diagonal). 4: ╲ (right-descending diagonal).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Horizontal-first establishes the "branch" line; vertical-first looks like 十 with extras attached.

mnemonic.summary: A line at the root of a tree (木) = ORIGIN / book.
mnemonic.visual: A tree (木) with a bar at the base — the ROOT of the tree, the ORIGIN, a BOOK at its core.
mnemonic.reading: ほん / もと / ぼん / ぽん — 日本 (Nihon, Japan), 本 (hon, book). Kun もと: 本 (moto, origin).
mnemonic.meaning: A line at the root of a tree (木) = ORIGIN / book.

sentences.ja: 日本語が できます。
sentences.translation_en: I can use Japanese. / I'm able to do (in) Japanese.

sentences.ja: わたしが 買った 本

stroke_order_trap.trap: Same as 木 + ONE extra short horizontal at the bottom (5 strokes total, NOT 4).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: identical to 木. 5: short horizontal across the lower vertical.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: The bottom horizontal converts 木 to 本; missing it is a common error.

mnemonic.summary: Like a tree growing toward you = COME.
mnemonic.visual: A person (人) under a tree — COME under the shade.
mnemonic.reading: らい / く / き / こ — 来る (kuru, to come). 来週 (raishuu, next week).
mnemonic.meaning: Like a tree growing toward you = COME.

sentences.ja: あした 来ますよね。
いつごろ 来ますか。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Two issues: (a) confusing the central structure with 末 (similar silhouette, different mid-element), and (b) stroking the central vertical before completing the top horizontal. Top horizontal first, then the central frame top-down.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: left vertical. 3: right vertical. 4-7: cross + dots inside.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 7 strokes. Distinguishes from 末 (similar but different element).

mnemonic.summary: Sun (日) rising behind a tree (木) = EAST.
mnemonic.visual: Sun (日) caught in a tree (木) — the sun rising in the EAST through the trees.
mnemonic.reading: とう / ひがし — 東京 (Toukyou, Tokyo, "Eastern capital"). Kun ひがし: 東 (higashi).
mnemonic.meaning: Sun (日) rising behind a tree (木) = EAST.

sentences.ja: 大阪から 東京に かえるのは あさっての よるです。
大阪から 東京に かえるのは あさっての よるです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top 一, then 田-like, then ノ+╲ legs.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: vertical. 3-7: 田 (box with cross). 8: ノ. 9: ╲.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 9 strokes. The horizontal+vertical at top precedes the box.

mnemonic.summary: Tree (木) where people meet (交) = SCHOOL.
mnemonic.visual: Tree (木) + 交 cross — buildings made of wood standing in CROSSED rows: a SCHOOL campus.
mnemonic.reading: こう — 学校 (gakkou). Always こう.
mnemonic.meaning: Tree (木) where people meet (交) = SCHOOL.

sentences.ja: 学校の あとで としょかんに 行きます。
sentences.translation_en: After school I go to the library.

sentences.ja: どうして 学校を 休みましたか。―あたまが いたかったからです。
sentences.translation_en: Why did you skip school? - Because I had a headache.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 木 first (left radical), then 交 right side.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: 木 (left half). 5: top dot. 6: top horizontal. 7-8: ハ inside. 9-10: bottom ノ + ╲.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 10 strokes. Left radical first.

mnemonic.summary: A figure of a nursing mother — the two emphasized dots originally depicted breasts, signaling "mother" by the act of nursing.
mnemonic.visual: A figure with two prominent dots — MOTHER with caring eyes (or feeding her child).
mnemonic.reading: ぼ / はは / かあ — Polite お母さん (okaasan), humble 母 (haha). On ぼ in 母国 (bokoku, motherland).
mnemonic.meaning: A figure of a nursing mother — the two emphasized dots originally depicted breasts, signaling "mother" by the act of nursing.

sentences.ja: 母に はなを あげます。
sentences.translation_en: I give flowers to my mother.

sentences.ja: 母と えいがを 見ました。
sentences.translation_en: I watched a movie with my mother.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 5 strokes. Outer box first, then horizontals.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical+hook. 2: top + right (bracket). 3: cross horizontal. 4: dot (inside). 5: dot (inside).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. The TWO dots inside are NOT a horizontal stroke.

mnemonic.summary: A person (人) over a mother (母) - every child has one = EVERY.
mnemonic.visual: A person bent forward + 母 mother — every mother bends to her child EVERY day.
mnemonic.reading: まい — only in compounds: 毎日 (mainichi, every day), 毎週 (maishuu, every week), 毎年 (maitoshi).
mnemonic.meaning: A person (人) over a mother (母) - every child has one = EVERY.

examples.reading: まいとし / まいねん
examples.gloss: every year (まいとし is more common in conversation; まいねん in formal/written contexts)

sentences.ja: わたしは 毎日 6時に おきます。
毎日 あさ こうえんで はしります。

n5_compounds.reading: まいとし / まいねん
n5_compounds.gloss: every year (まいとし is more common in conversation; ま

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ at top, then horizontal, then 母-like below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (top-left descending). 2: horizontal. 3-6: 母-like bottom shape.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes; distinguishes from 海 (has water radical).

mnemonic.summary: Steam (气) rising = SPIRIT / mood / air.
mnemonic.visual: Steam rising — the air ENERGY surrounding things.
mnemonic.reading: き / け — 元気 (genki, energetic), 天気 (tenki, weather), 病気 (byouki, sickness).
mnemonic.meaning: Steam (气) rising = SPIRIT / mood / air.

examples.gloss: feeling / mood (気持ち; 持 in kana per K-1)

sentences.ja: 天気は とても よかったです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top 气 frame first, then 米 inside.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: 气 frame (top arc + hook). 5-6: ノ + ╲. 7-6: 〆-like inside.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes.

mnemonic.summary: A central drop with splashes = WATER.
mnemonic.visual: A central drop with splashes either side — WATER spreading on impact.
mnemonic.reading: すい / みず — Wednesday is すいようび. Kun みず is the base word for "water."
mnemonic.meaning: A central drop with splashes = WATER.

sentences.ja: 月よう日と 水よう日と 金よう日に カフェで アルバイトを して います。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Many learners write the central vertical first.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: hook (left curve+drop). 2: small dot (left). 3: long downward sweep right. 4: hook (right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Wrong order produces unbalanced left/right halves; correct order makes the kanji look like flowing water.

mnemonic.summary: Flames flickering upward = FIRE.
mnemonic.visual: Sparks flying outward from a central point — FIRE leaping with two side flames.
mnemonic.reading: か / ひ — Tuesday is かようび. Kun ひ: 火事 (kaji, fire emergency) but also ひ: 火 (hi, fire).
mnemonic.meaning: Flames flickering upward = FIRE.

sentences.ja: 女: 火よう日と 木よう日です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Some write the central X first instead of the side dots.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot. 2: right dot. 3: long descending-left stroke. 4: long descending-right stroke.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Side dots first establishes the "spark" baseline; correct order also matches the radical 灬 position.

mnemonic.summary: Two crossing strokes - paternal authority figure = FATHER.
mnemonic.visual: Two crossed arms over a base — FATHER holding everything together.
mnemonic.reading: ふ / ちち / とう — Polite お父さん (otousan), humble 父 (chichi). On ふ in 父親 (chichioya).
mnemonic.meaning: Two crossing strokes - paternal authority figure = FATHER.

sentences.ja: 父に とけいを もらいました。
sentences.translation_en: I received a watch from my father.

sentences.ja: 父が プレゼントを くれました。
sentences.translation_en: My father gave me a present.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Two top dots first (left then right), then long X.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot (descending). 2: right dot (descending). 3: long ノ (down-left). 4: long ╲ (down-right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Top dots establish position; without them, 父 looks like 文 (literature, N4).

mnemonic.summary: A plant sprouting from the soil = LIFE / birth.
mnemonic.visual: A sprout pushing through the earth — LIFE BEING BORN.
mnemonic.reading: せい / しょう / い / う / なま — 学生 (gakusei, student), 生まれる (umareru, to be born). Hugely flexible kanji.
mnemonic.meaning: A plant sprouting from the soil = LIFE / birth.

sentences.ja: 学生が 5にん います。
先生に 花を あげました。
sentences.translation_en: I gave flowers to the teacher.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal, then short ノ, then more horizontals, then vertical.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: top horizontal. 3: short horizontal. 4: vertical. 5: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. Similar to 先 but different element.

mnemonic.summary: A grid of irrigation channels = RICE PADDY.
mnemonic.visual: A grid of four rice paddies — RICE FIELDS divided by levees.
mnemonic.reading: でん / た / だ — common in surnames: 田中 (Tanaka), 山田 (Yamada).
mnemonic.meaning: A grid of irrigation channels = RICE PADDY.

sentences.ja: 田中さんは 「行きません」と 言いました。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Inside cross stroke comes BEFORE the bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top + right (one bracket). 3: middle horizontal. 4: middle vertical. 5: bottom horizontal (closes box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Closing the box LAST is a Japanese convention for box-shapes; otherwise the inside strokes become cramped.

mnemonic.summary: Power (力) in the rice field (田) = MAN.
mnemonic.visual: Field (田) on top + power (力) below — a MAN providing POWER to the FIELD.
mnemonic.reading: だん / おとこ — 男の人 (otoko no hito, man). Also 男性 (dansei, male).
mnemonic.meaning: Power (力) in the rice field (田) = MAN.

sentences.ja: 男の人と 女の人が 話しています。
女の人が 男の人に 電話しています。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Two common errors: (a) writing 力 first (left-to-right scan instinct), and (b) drawing 田 with the inner cross out of order. The cross inside 田 always comes after the outer box closes.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-5: 田 (top half, 5 strokes). 6-7: 力 (hook + ノ).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Component-by-component: 田 fully before 力.

mnemonic.summary: A claw (釆) ordering rice paddies (田) by NUMBER.
mnemonic.visual: Rice (米) + field (田) — taking your TURN in the rice field.
mnemonic.reading: ばん — 番号 (bangou, number), 一番 (ichiban, #1, first). Always ばん.
mnemonic.meaning: A claw (釆) ordering rice paddies (田) by NUMBER.

sentences.ja: 一番 大きい 本です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 采 (top, 7 strokes) + 田 (5 strokes).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-7: 采 top (米-like). 8-12: 田.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 12 strokes.

mnemonic.summary: A ray (丿) shining on the sun (日) = WHITE.
mnemonic.visual: A simple kanji with a tail — a candle's WHITE flame casting light.
mnemonic.reading: はく / びゃく / しろ / しら — 白い (shiroi, white). 白人 (hakujin, white person).
mnemonic.meaning: A ray (丿) shining on the sun (日) = WHITE.

sentences.ja: わたしの いえには 大きい 白い いぬが います。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Omitting the leading ノ turns 白 into 日 — a different kanji entirely. The short ノ at the very top is stroke #1, and is what distinguishes 白 from 日 visually and orthographically.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (top). 2: left vertical. 3: top + right (bracket). 4: middle horizontal. 5: bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. The leading ノ distinguishes from 日.

mnemonic.summary: One (一) over white (白) = a HUNDRED whitewashed walls.
mnemonic.visual: One (一) stacked on white (白) — the FIRST WHITE coin: HUNDRED yen.
mnemonic.reading: ひゃく — 100 of anything. Watch for sound shift: 三百 (sanbyaku), 六百 (roppyaku), 八百 (happyaku).
mnemonic.meaning: One (一) over white (白) = a HUNDRED whitewashed walls.

sentences.ja: B: 千五百円でした。
この 本は 千五百円です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal first (the "hat"), then 白 below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: ノ stroke into 白. 3-6: 白 (left vertical, top+right bracket, middle horizontal, bottom horizontal).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Hat-first; then standard 白 closure rule.

mnemonic.summary: A vertical eye with pupil-bars = EYE.
mnemonic.visual: A vertical eye, the iris in the middle — EYE rotated 90°.
mnemonic.reading: もく / め — Kun め: 目 (me, eye), very common. 目的 (mokuteki, purpose) for the on.
mnemonic.meaning: A vertical eye with pupil-bars = EYE.

sentences.ja: 目が きれいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Box-closure rule: left vertical, top+right bracket, two horizontals inside, bottom close.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top + right (bracket). 3: middle-1 horizontal. 4: middle-2 horizontal. 5: bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. Box-rule: enclose first, then fill, close last.

mnemonic.summary: Altar (礻) on the earth (土) where people gather = COMPANY / shrine.
mnemonic.visual: Spirit altar (示) + 土 earth — the COMPANY shrine where workers pray to the earth-god.
mnemonic.reading: しゃ — 会社 (kaisha, company), 社員 (shain, employee). Always しゃ.
mnemonic.meaning: Altar (礻) on the earth (土) where people gather = COMPANY / shrine.

sentences.ja: ちちは <u>会社員</u> です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: The left radical 示 (ねへん) must finish completely before 土 starts. A common slip is interleaving — drawing 示's leftmost ノ, then jumping to 土's top horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: 示 (left half: dot, two horizontals, two ノ). 5-7: 土.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 7 strokes. Left radical 示 first.

mnemonic.summary: My (厶) grain (禾) = PRIVATE / I.
mnemonic.visual: Rice (禾) + 厶 private — your PRIVATE rice supply: I, ME.
mnemonic.reading: し / わたし / わたくし — 私 (watashi, I). On し in 私生活 (shiseikatsu, private life).
mnemonic.meaning: My (厶) grain (禾) = PRIVATE / I.

sentences.ja: 私は 学生 では （　　）。
私は 中国へ 行った こと（　　）あります。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 禾 (rice-plant radical, 5 strokes) on left + 厶 right.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: ノ (top). 2: top horizontal. 3: vertical. 4: ノ left. 5: ╲ right. 6-7: 厶 (right side).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 7 strokes. Left radical 禾 sets the meaning (originally "my rice plant").

mnemonic.summary: A cave (穴) of work (工) - empty above = SKY / empty.
mnemonic.visual: Cave (穴) + 工 work — the EMPTY SKY carved out above.
mnemonic.reading: くう / そら / から / あ — 空 (sora, sky), 空気 (kuuki, air), 空く (aku, to become empty).
mnemonic.meaning: A cave (穴) of work (工) - empty above = SKY / empty.

sentences.ja: 空が 青いです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Learners often start 工 before closing 穴 on top. The 穴 cap (5 strokes) is a single self-contained unit that must finish first — same hat-first rule as 究 / 突.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-5: 穴 (top hat structure). 6-8: 工.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes. Hat-first structure.

mnemonic.summary: A person planted on the ground = STAND.
mnemonic.visual: A person standing tall on the ground — STAND UP straight.
mnemonic.reading: りつ / りゅう / た — 立つ (tatsu, to stand). 国立 (kokuritsu, national).
mnemonic.meaning: A person planted on the ground = STAND.

examples.gloss: entry / entering (often "立入禁止" = no entry)

sentences.ja: ですから 父は いつも 立って います。
ですから 父は いつも 立って います。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top dot, then short horizontal, then ハ-legs, then long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top dot. 2: top horizontal. 3-4: middle ハ (two short strokes). 5: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes; mid-section ハ first then long base. Order ensures stable balance.

mnemonic.summary: Ear (耳) at the gate (門) = HEAR.
mnemonic.visual: Gate (門) + ear (耳) — putting your EAR to the GATE to LISTEN.
mnemonic.reading: ぶん / もん / き — 聞く (kiku, to listen/ask). 新聞 (shinbun, newspaper).
mnemonic.meaning: Ear (耳) at the gate (門) = HEAR.

examples.gloss: be audible / can be heard (intransitive of 聞く)

sentences.ja: おんがくを 聞きながら べんきょうします。
sentences.translation_en: I study while listening to music.

n5_compounds.gloss: be audible / can be heard (intransitive of 聞く)

stroke_order_trap.trap: 門 (gate) frame OUTSIDE first, then 耳 (ear) INSIDE.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-8: 門 (gate frame: left half, then right half — each 4 strokes). 9-14: 耳 (ear) inside.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Universal enclosed-kanji rule: outside-frame first, inside last.

mnemonic.summary: Grass (艹) transforming (化) = FLOWER.
mnemonic.visual: Grass (艹) + 化 change — a sprout CHANGED into a FLOWER.
mnemonic.reading: か / はな — 花 (hana, flower). 花見 (hanami, flower viewing).
mnemonic.meaning: Grass (艹) transforming (化) = FLOWER.

sentences.ja: 先生に 花を あげました。
sentences.translation_en: I gave flowers to the teacher.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 艹 (grass radical, 3 strokes) on top, then 化 below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 艹 (3-stroke grass radical). 4-5: 亻 (person on left of 化). 6-7: 匕 (right of 化).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 7 strokes. Top-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: Two walking-man halves = GO.
mnemonic.visual: A crossroads viewed from above — choose where to GO.
mnemonic.reading: こう / ぎょう / い / おこな — 行く (iku, to go). 銀行 (ginkou, bank), 行事 (gyouji, event).
mnemonic.meaning: Two walking-man halves = GO.

examples.gloss: way of going / how to get there

sentences.ja: いま 行きます。
どう 行きますか。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 彳 radical (3 strokes) on the left FIRST, then 亍 right side.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 彳 left side (short descending, dot, long vertical). 4-6: right side (short, long, vertical-hook).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Radical-first is universal; common error is drawing both sides interleaved.

mnemonic.summary: A bird returning to its nest at sunset = WEST.
mnemonic.visual: A bird-cage at sunset — birds returning to the cage in the WEST.
mnemonic.reading: せい / さい / にし — 西 (nishi, west). 西洋 (seiyou, the West/Western).
mnemonic.meaning: A bird returning to its nest at sunset = WEST.

sentences.ja: 学校は 西に あります。
sentences.translation_en: The school is to the west.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal, then 一 inside box, then box close.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: short horizontal inside (top). 3: left vertical. 4: top+right (bracket). 5: middle horizontal+legs. 6: bottom horizontal (closes).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes. Variant of box-closure rule.

mnemonic.summary: Eye (目) on legs (儿) - looking around = SEE.
mnemonic.visual: An eye (目) on legs (儿) — eyes that walk around to SEE everything.
mnemonic.reading: けん / み — 見る (miru, to see/watch). 意見 (iken, opinion).
mnemonic.meaning: Eye (目) on legs (儿) - looking around = SEE.

examples.gloss: to see off (見送る; 送 in kana per K-1)

sentences.ja: 見るだけです。
よく テレビを 見ます。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 目 first (5 strokes), then 儿 (legs) at the bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-5: 目 (eye). 6: bottom-left ノ. 7: bottom-right hook.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Eye-first then legs; common error is drawing legs first to "anchor" the kanji.

mnemonic.summary: Words (言) leaving the mouth = SAY.
mnemonic.visual: A mouth with words coming out in layered lines — SPEAKING multiple ideas.
mnemonic.reading: げん / ごん / い / こと — 言う (iu, to say). 言葉 (kotoba, word), 言語 (gengo, language).
mnemonic.meaning: Words (言) leaving the mouth = SAY.

sentences.ja: もう いちど 言ってください。
ともだちは 行くと 言いました。
sentences.translation_en: My friend said he would go.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top "stack" of 3 horizontals, then 口 below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal (top). 2: middle horizontal. 3: longest horizontal. 4-7: 口 box (with closure rule).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 7 strokes. Top stack + 口 universal for the 言 radical.

mnemonic.summary: Speech (言) using the tongue (舌) = TALK / story.
mnemonic.visual: Speech (言) + tongue (舌) — words spoken with the tongue: TALK.
mnemonic.reading: わ / はな — 話す (hanasu, to talk). 電話 (denwa, phone), 会話 (kaiwa, conversation).
mnemonic.meaning: Speech (言) using the tongue (舌) = TALK / story.

sentences.ja: 日本語を 話す ことが できます。
にほんごを 話すのは むずかしいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Drawing 舌 (the right element) before completing 言 (the left radical, ごんべん). All radical-on-left kanji follow left-first: finish 言 entirely, then start 舌.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 13 strokes. Left-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: Speech (言) of myself (吾) = LANGUAGE.
mnemonic.visual: Speech (言) + 五 five + 口 mouth — five mouths SPEAKING a LANGUAGE.
mnemonic.reading: ご — 日本語 (nihongo), 英語 (eigo). Always ご.
mnemonic.meaning: Speech (言) of myself (吾) = LANGUAGE.

sentences.ja: 日本語が すこし わかります。
日本語の 本を おねがいします。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 言 radical first (left, 7 strokes), then 吾 right.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 14 strokes. Left-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: Speech (言) selling (売) ideas = READ.
mnemonic.visual: Speech (言) + 売 sell — READING ALOUD to sell the words to the audience.
mnemonic.reading: どく / よ — 読む (yomu, to read). 読書 (dokusho, reading books).
mnemonic.meaning: Speech (言) selling (売) ideas = READ.

examples.gloss: way of reading; reading (of a kanji)
reading material (読み物; 物 in kana per K-1)

sentences.ja: ほんを 読んで、ねます。
ほんを 読んで、ねます。
sentences.translation_en: I'll read a book and sleep.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 言 (left) + 売-like (right). 14 strokes.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 14 strokes. Left-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: Net (四) for shell-money (貝) = BUY.
mnemonic.visual: A net (罒) over shell-money (貝) — netting up coins to BUY something.
mnemonic.reading: ばい / か — 買う (kau, to buy). 売買 (baibai, buying-and-selling).
mnemonic.meaning: Net (四) for shell-money (貝) = BUY.

sentences.ja: ペンや ノートなどを 買いました。
sentences.translation_en: I bought pens, notebooks, and so on.

sentences.ja: デパートへ 買いものに 行きます。
sentences.translation_en: I'm going to the department store to shop.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 罒 (eye-like cap) first, then 貝.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 12 strokes. Cap-first.

mnemonic.summary: Mouth-shape (口) over stop (止) = FOOT (where you stop).
mnemonic.visual: Top mouth + bottom path — using your FEET to walk and TALK.
mnemonic.reading: そく / あし / た — 足 (ashi, foot/leg). 足りる (tariru, to be enough).
mnemonic.meaning: Mouth-shape (口) over stop (止) = FOOT (where you stop).

examples.gloss: footprints (足跡; 跡 written in kana per K-1)

sentences.ja: 足が いたいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 口 first (top), then 止-like leg shape below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 口 (top box). 4: horizontal. 5: vertical. 6: ノ. 7: ╲.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 7 strokes. Component-by-component.

mnemonic.summary: A wheel with axle and bed = VEHICLE / car.
mnemonic.visual: A wagon viewed from above — the body and two wheels of a CAR.
mnemonic.reading: しゃ / くるま — 自動車 (jidousha, automobile), 車 (kuruma, car).
mnemonic.meaning: A wheel with axle and bed = VEHICLE / car.

sentences.ja: でも、電車が おくれて、八時十分に 学校に つきました。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Outer frame middle vertical comes LAST.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2-3: 日 frame (left-vert, top+right bracket, middle-horiz). 4: bottom horizontal of 日 (closes box). 5: middle vertical of 田-frame extending up. 6-7: bottom shape.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Like 中, the central vertical is drawn LAST as a unifying axis.

mnemonic.summary: Walking (辶) around (周) the calendar = WEEK.
mnemonic.visual: Walking radical (⻌) + 周 around — a WEEK is what you walk around in seven days.
mnemonic.reading: しゅう — 今週 (konshuu, this week), 来週 (raishuu, next week). Always しゅう.
mnemonic.meaning: Walking (辶) around (周) the calendar = WEEK.

sentences.ja: 今は 一週間に 二かい、ピアノきょうしつに 行きます。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 辶 LAST: write 周 first, then add 辶 path below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-8: 周 (interior + outer frame). 9-11: 辶 (path-radical, the LAST stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 辶 radical is always written LAST in 辶-kanji.

mnemonic.summary: Walking (辶) with one's head (首) up = ROAD / way.
mnemonic.visual: Walking radical + neck (首) — keeping your NECK aimed at the ROAD ahead.
mnemonic.reading: どう / みち — 道 (michi, road), 北海道 (Hokkaidou). 茶道 (sadou, tea ceremony "way").
mnemonic.meaning: Walking (辶) with one's head (首) up = ROAD / way.

sentences.ja: でも、道が こんで います。
道が わかりません。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 辶 LAST: write 首 first, then add 辶.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-9: 首. 10-12: 辶 (path-radical, the LAST 3 strokes).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 辶 radical is always written LAST.

mnemonic.summary: A person (人) over a king (王) with two specks of gold = METAL/GOLD.
mnemonic.visual: A roof with two diamonds inside — GOLD nuggets hidden under the roof of a cave.
mnemonic.reading: きん / かね — Friday is きんようび. Kun かね: お金 (okane, money).
mnemonic.meaning: A person (人) over a king (王) with two specks of gold = METAL/GOLD.

examples.gloss: golden time / sweet potato dish (named after a folk hero)

sentences.ja: お金が ないので、買えません。
sentences.translation_en: I have no money, so I can't buy it.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 人-hat first (top dots), then horizontals, then 王 below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-2: 人-shape (left ノ, right ╲ — converging hat). 3-4: two horizontals (top). 5: vertical. 6: middle horizontal. 7: ノ. 8: ╲ (bottom diagonals).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes. Hat-first convention applied to a 人 element.

mnemonic.summary: Long flowing hair on an elder = LONG.
mnemonic.visual: A figure with long flowing hair down the back — LONG and the HEAD/CHIEF.
mnemonic.reading: ちょう / なが — 長い (nagai, long). 社長 (shachou, company president).
mnemonic.meaning: Long flowing hair on an elder = LONG.

sentences.ja: この みちは <u>長い</u> です。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top 一 + complex middle + 衣-like bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2-7: middle strokes. 8: ノ (bottom). Total 8.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes. Common confusion: missing the middle short horizontal.

mnemonic.summary: Sun (日) shining through a gate (門) = INTERVAL / between.
mnemonic.visual: Gate (門) with sun (日) inside — the SPACE BETWEEN two walls where light enters.
mnemonic.reading: かん / けん / あいだ / ま — 時間 (jikan, time-space), 間 (aida, between).
mnemonic.meaning: Sun (日) shining through a gate (門) = INTERVAL / between.

sentences.ja: 1時間ぐらい まちました。
行きたいけど、時間が ありません。
sentences.translation_en: I want to go, but I don't have time.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 門 (gate frame) outside, then 日 inside.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-8: 門 (gate: left half 4 strokes, right half 4 strokes). 9-12: 日 (inside box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 12 strokes. Frame-outside, inside-inside; standard enclosure rule.

mnemonic.summary: Drops falling from a cloud-shaped roof = RAIN.
mnemonic.visual: A roof with four drops falling — RAIN coming down outside.
mnemonic.reading: う / あめ — 雨 (ame, rain). 大雨 (ooame, heavy rain).
mnemonic.meaning: Drops falling from a cloud-shaped roof = RAIN.

sentences.ja: あした 雨だろう。
あした 雨でしょう。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal+box, then 4 dots arranged.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: left ノ. 3: vertical+hook bracket. 4-7: 4 inside dots.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes. Top frame first, dots inside last.

mnemonic.summary: Rain (雨) hitting a paddy (田) - lightning = ELECTRICITY.
mnemonic.visual: Rain (雨) + 田 lightning streak — ELECTRICITY: lightning in the rain.
mnemonic.reading: でん — 電車 (densha, train), 電話 (denwa, phone), 電気 (denki, electricity).
mnemonic.meaning: Rain (雨) hitting a paddy (田) - lightning = ELECTRICITY.

sentences.ja: でも、電車が おくれて、八時十分に 学校に つきました。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 雨 cap (4-stroke + 4 dots), then 田 with 乚 extension.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-8: 雨 cap. 9-12: 田-like body. 13: hook below.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 13 strokes. 雨 always tops the kanji.

mnemonic.summary: A roof over good things to eat = EAT.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over rice — sheltering the FOOD beneath.
mnemonic.reading: しょく / く / た — 食べる (taberu, to eat), 食事 (shokuji, meal).
mnemonic.meaning: A roof over good things to eat = EAT.

sentences.ja: ごはん 食べないの？
ぜんぜん 食べません。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 人-hat on top, then 良-like below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left ノ. 2: right ╲. 3-9: 良 below.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 9 strokes.

mnemonic.summary: Food (飠) and a yawn (欠) - DRINK to wash it down.
mnemonic.visual: Eat-radical (食) + 欠 lack — you lack water, so you DRINK.
mnemonic.reading: いん / の — 飲む (nomu, to drink), 飲み物 (nomimono, drink).
mnemonic.meaning: Food (飠) and a yawn (欠) - DRINK to wash it down.

sentences.ja: コーヒーだけ 飲みます。
コーヒーが 飲みたいです。

stroke_order_trap.trap: 食 (left, 9 strokes) + 欠 (right, 4 strokes).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-9: 食 radical. 10-13: 欠 (man with open mouth).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 13 strokes. Left-radical-first; 食 used in many food/drink verbs.

mnemonic.summary: Horse (馬) measured (尺) - a relay STATION.
mnemonic.visual: Horse (馬) + 尺 measure — STATIONS are where horses were measured/changed.
mnemonic.reading: えき — 駅 (eki, station). 東京駅 (Toukyou-eki). Always えき.
mnemonic.meaning: Horse (馬) measured (尺) - a relay STATION.

sentences.ja: 駅の まえに あります。
sentences.translation_en: It is in front of the station. (noun-modifier の: links a location to a positional noun)

stroke_order_trap.trap: 馬 (horse radical, 10 strokes) on left, then 尺 right.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 14 strokes. Left-radical-first.

mnemonic.summary: A tall pavilion silhouette = TALL / expensive.
mnemonic.visual: A tall pagoda silhouette — TALL/HIGH/EXPENSIVE building.
mnemonic.reading: こう / たか — 高い (takai, tall/expensive). 高校 (koukou, high school).
mnemonic.meaning: A tall pavilion silhouette = TALL / expensive.

sentences.ja: あの 山は 高いですか。

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top "hat" first (with dot), then middle 口, then bottom 冂 with 口 inside.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top dot. 2: top horizontal. 3-5: middle 口 (3 strokes). 6-7: bottom 冂 frame. 8-10: inside 口 (3 strokes). Total 10.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Multi-tier kanji: each tier from top to bottom; within each tier follow box rule.
